rationale: The core-0.34 candidate is expected to fail EXACTLY the class-(c) golden/version assertions (the taxonomy §4.2 inventory) and NOTHING else. compare-spike-runs.js --mode classify reads the candidate's `npm test` and `test:browser` vitest --reporter=json output and enforces FOUR layers: (1) the observed failing (file -> count) set equals `node`/`browser` byFile exactly; (2) ASSERTION-LEVEL failure signatures — every failing assertion in an expected-red file must match an allowed {titleSubstring, messageRegex} signature at its exact count, so a failure MOVING to a different assertion inside the same red test (e.g. the staleness test's trace-version/record-size contracts, which precede its engine-version pin) is caught even at constant per-file count; (3) must-stay-green titles never appear in the failing set; (4) POSITIVE presence — the must-pass titles appear with status 'passed' at exact multiplicity (absence-of-failure cannot distinguish passed from renamed/skipped). Schema /2 adds layers 2 and 4 plus nodeChromiumRequiredKeys (the exact required cross-env digest set for --mode invariants). See docs/engine-assertion-taxonomy-2026-07.md §4.2 and docs/rapier-034-spike-2026-07.md §4.

node.reporter: npm test (vitest run --reporter=json), Node, deterministic + ordinary flavors, tests/browser/** excluded

tests/evaluation-determinism.test.js.asserts: rapierVersion staleness pin (0.19.3 -> 0.19.3-c13133ad.0) + eval-A/B/C/D golden trace digests

allowedFailureSignatures.why: the test checks trace/fixture versions, recordBytes, step counts, dt, and fnv1a consistency BEFORE the engine pin — only the engine-version assertion (its committed custom message) is the allowed failure point
each golden fixture must fail at the checkpoint-divergence formatter (physics moved), never at the structural recordCount/byteCount assertions that precede it
the allowed failure is the checkpoint divergence (or its digest backstop against the committed 5a219735), never the lock-internal-consistency or recordCount/byteCount contracts before it

tests/population-determinism.test.js.asserts: rapierVersion pin, fitness-vector digest (bded0d30 -> measured), champion solo-trace digest

allowedFailureSignatures.why: 15+ policy/schema/world-mode/seed/dt contracts precede the engine pin in this test — only the engine-version assertion (its committed custom message) may fail

this test's FIRST half is internal repeatability (a===b, MUST hold); the allowed failure is only the second half's comparison against the committed fitness-vector digest bded0d30 — a repeatability failure prints 'expected false to be true' and does NOT match

allowedFailureSignatures.messageRegex: champion trace: first divergent checkpoint index|to be 'f5c5f0c7'
allowedFailureSignatures.why: allowed failure is the champion-trace checkpoint divergence (or its digest backstop against the committed f5c5f0c7), never the structural count assertions before it

tests/bench-schema.test.js.asserts: report.meta.rapier == package.json dependency pin { compat: '0.19.3', deterministicCompat: '0.19.3' }

allowedFailureSignatures.why: the meta.rapier toEqual expected side always prints the 'deterministicCompat' key — unique to that assertion in this test regardless of how the diff wraps; the schema/os/cpu/fixture assertions before it never mention it
the rapierVersion .toBe('0.19.3') failure message ends the expected side with the quoted bare pin; the schema/dt/hard-check assertions around it never print that literal

node.mustPassRationale: The failing-set exclusion (mustPassAssertionSubstrings) plus the POSITIVE presence requirement (mustPassPresent: each title observed with status 'passed' at exact multiplicity — e.g. FOUR passed 'two fresh worlds agree' fixtures) together enforce that the internal-determinism gate-(a) checks and the pure/structural teeth actually RAN and PASSED on the candidate. Every substring was verified unique to a single Node test file, so whole-report counting cannot double-count.

browser.reporter: npm run test:browser (vitest run --config vitest.browser.config.js --reporter=json), pinned Chromium
browser.note: The local historical spike SKIPPED test:browser, so per-file browser counts are FIRST MEASURED by the heavy CI dispatch (C5); tighten this section from that run's observed failing set (counts + allowedFailureSignatures). The load-bearing cross-env check does not wait for that: --mode invariants requires the COMPLETE nodeChromiumRequiredKeys set to be extracted from BOTH environments with equal values.

tests/browser/evaluation-determinism.tes.asserts: A-D golden trace digests, in Chromium

tests/browser/population-determinism.tes.asserts: population fitness-vector + champion-trace digests, in Chromium

browser.mustPassRationale: The Chromium population 'pure initializer locks' test hashes PURE genotype/initializer data (no physics), so it MUST run and pass on the candidate — enforced by presence even while browser per-file counts remain bootstrap (null).

nodeChromiumRequiredKeysRationale: The EXACT cross-env digest set --mode invariants enforces: every key must be extracted from BOTH the Node and Chromium determinism-failure printouts and carry the same measured value; a missing key in either environment, a value disagreement, or an extracted key OUTSIDE this set all fail (set equality, both directions — Node extracting A-D while Chromium extracts only A must not pass on A's agreement alone). These six are all the semantic digests that move on the candidate and exist in both environments; the Node-only cold-module test (evaluation-golden.test.js) is deliberately not a member and is excluded by the extractor's determinism-file filter.

mustPassInvariants.dtReadback: world.timestep reads back Math.fround(1/60) = 0.01666666753590107 on the candidate (the probe/runner dt tooth stays GREEN — else the whole physics suite cascade-reds for the wrong reason)
mustPassInvariants.internalDeterminismGateA: the determinism gate's 'two fresh worlds agree on digest + every checkpoint' (internal repeatability) PASSES on the candidate for A-D; only gate (d) 'matches committed lock' is allowed to move — enforced by mustPassPresent, not just absence-of-failure
mustPassInvariants.nodeChromiumAgreement: the candidate's MEASURED digests are identical between the Node and Chromium determinism-gate failure printouts for the COMPLETE nodeChromiumRequiredKeys set (cross-env determinism holds on the new engine)

timing.gate: compare-spike-runs.js --mode timing parses the candidate's probe:timing log: every `DRIFT <check name>` line must name a check in allowedDriftChecks (any OTHER drift fails the arm — ten unrelated API-contract drifts must not hide behind one expected exit 1), and the probe's exit code must be consistent with the parsed DRIFT lines (exit 0 with drifts, or exit 1 with none, is a parse/crash inconsistency and fails). An allowlisted drift that VANISHED (probe fully green) is reported loudly as a class-(b) flip-watch, not fatal — the drift is recorded, not a contract.
timing.note: the single class-(b) engine-finding the local run saw (exit 1 on probe:timing, 36 checks / 1 DRIFT, adoption-critical sub-checks green: dt readback, isValid/isSleeping, timing member surface)

heavyEvidence.rationale: The DECLARED coverage of a heavy=true run, enforced by compare()'s arm-usability check on BOTH arms: `--pass prevalence` (defaultConfig, no --smoke) generates the three Phase-1A characterization masters each with 20 unique individuals, and `--prevalence-seeds 20260730` generates the fresh seed with 20. A one-row / missing-seed / missing-fresh-seed prevalence artifact must NOT participate in a citable heavy verdict — the scientific catastrophic COUNTS stay observations, but the experiment's declared coverage (which seeds, how many individuals) is a contract. Derived from scripts/probe-physics-explosion.js smokeConfig/defaultConfig (populationSize 20).

bootstrapCompleteRationale: The TWO-STAGE citability gate (machine-enforced, not narrated). While false: the browser byFile inventory is NOT finalized (null counts, no allowedFailureSignatures), so an unrelated browser-contract regression in an expected-red browser file cannot be caught — therefore compare() forces `citable=false` on EVERY run, no matter how clean. The first heavy=true dispatch is the BOOTSTRAP run: it produces the browser observed failing set; a human commits those counts + signatures into the browser section AND flips this flag to true; only a SECOND heavy run (against the finalized inventory) can be citable. This makes 'the browser inventory was finalized before we certified no-hidden-regression' a structural precondition, not a promise. `titlesPendingFirstHeavyRun` is the human-readable twin of this flag.
